ACT _ONE

sS conch

FADE IN: ON SCREEN

We see a fat blue-collar DINOSAUR lecturing his spike headed SON.

DINOSAUR DAD Turn off that rock ‘n’ rock music! DINOSAUR SON Get with it dad. This is the Stone Age. PULL BACK TO: INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - LIVING ROOM = NIGHT

The Simpsons, except Marge, watch TV. They are dressed for dinner. BART and HOMER LAUGH together.

LISA (CHUCKLING) These talking dinosaurs are more real than most real families on TV. ON TV

The BABY DINOSAUR is WHACKING the father on the head with a frying pan.

BACK TO SCENE

Homer has MAGGIE on his lap.

8F20 REVISED TABLE DRAFT 9/20/91 2. San HOMER co (CHUCKLING) Look Maggie, they have a baby, too. BART It’s like they saw our lives and put it right up on screen. MARGE enters with PATTY. MARGE Now kids, I know you’re all excited about meeting Aunt Selma’s new boyfriend tonight. HOMER Pfft. MARGE But Patty just told me something about him that’s a little unpleasant and I thought I should warn you. MONTAGE

The Simpsons picture what could be wrong with Selma’s boyfriend.

A.) Lisa pictures Aunt Selma walking in with the ELEPHANT MAN on her arm.

B.) Homer pictures Aunt Selma wheeling in a smiling head on a tray with tubes coming out of the neck.

Cc.) Bart pictures Aunt Selma walking in with Homer on her arm.

8F20 REVISED TABLE DRAFT 9/20/91 3. Benn! BACK TO SCENE te PATTY I’1l put this as delicately as I can. He’s a jailbird. MARGE Patty, he’s an ex-convict. He’s paid his debt to society. PATTY Then how come you’re not using the good silverware? MARGE I’m just not. SFX: DOOR BELL = Seamed Marge opens the door. SELMA and her well-dressed DATE enter. Her date removes his hat, revealing a large shock of red hair. SIDESHOW BOB Good evening, all. Why, hello, Bart. BART/LISA (SCREAM) Sideshow Bob! They run and hide behind Marge. SELMA Forgive their manners. They haven‘t had any kind of a father. SIDESHOW BOB No, Selma dear, I’m afraid the children’s reaction is quite

understandable.

FOS :

He pats Bart’s head. SIDESHOW BOB (CONT’D) You see, Bart here is the Shamus in short pants who sent me to prison. HOMER (GASPS) Of course! Now I remember...

BART Au revoir, suckers! The wagon drives away.

INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - LIVING ROOM SIDESHOW BOB (PUZZLED) No, I’ve never been to France... HOMER (SNAPS FINGERS) Oh, now I know!

EDDIE and LOU cart off GROUNDSKEEPER WILLIE. (USE FOOTAGE FROM “SEPARATE VOCATIONS".)

EXT. KRUSTY STUDIOS - 1990 The police lead SIDESHOW BOB to the paddy wagon. SIDESHOW BOB And I would have gotten away with it, if not for those meddling kids! BART Take him away, boys. The crowd CHEERS as Sideshow Bob is driven away.

HOMER Gee, if some snot-nosed little kid sent me to prison, first thing out, I‘d find out where he lives, and tear

him a new bellybutton.

Homer picks up a knife and GRUNTS as he makes a! motion. BART (WEAK LAUGH)

Bart tugs

at his collar nervously.

SIDESHOW BOB (CHUCKLES) Ahh, Mr. Simpson, you’re forgetting the first two noble truths of the Buddha.

HOMER I am not.

SIDESHOW BOB Yes, you are. One: existence is suffering. Two: the cause of suffering is desire. In this case, my desire to do high-quality children’s programing.

AWARDS SHOW SET - EVENING a Krusty stands on a stage featuring a giant award. SIDESHOW BOB (V.0) Although I was named best children’s show sidekick at the Daytime Emny Awards, my bitterness ruined what should have been the greatest night of my life. KRUSTY The nominees are: Droopy Drawers; Colonel Coward; Sideshow Bob; and Suck-up the Vacuum. We see mortised around Krusty: a homely clown with big oversized underwear, sitting next to a beautiful woman in an evening gown; a nervous military man pulling his hair

out and biting his nails; Sideshow Bob behind bars; and a black and white picture of a vacuum cleaner.

INT. PRISON - CELLBLOCK Sark

The guards lead a prisoner down a row of incredibly overcrowded cells. They open the door to the broom closet. There are three inmates inside. The guards toss the prisoner in and lock the door. PAN OVER to six inmates sharing Bob’s cell. SIDESHOW BOB (V.O.) In our over-crowded cell, we became little more than beasts. SIDESHOW BOB Who used my chapstick? CELLMATE #1 I did. Here you go. SIDESHOW BOB (TIGHTLY) I don’t want it. INT. PRISON - LICENSE PLATE FACTORY SIDESHOW BOB (V.O.) My only joy came with plotting a horrible revenge against the boy who

put me there...

Like the lone crocus that pokes

through the prison yard, our love

bloomed despite all obstacles. Sideshow Bob and Selma look lovingly at each other, separated by bullet-proof glass. Next to them another prison COUPLE kiss each other with the glass between then.

Selma, may I? He puckers and leans towards the glass. SELMA

(SHAKES HEAD) Uh-uh. On the cheek.

She presses her cheek against the glass, contorting it

Marge, with flowers in her hair, is taping the wedding with a video camera. She sees Krusty The Clown eating alone at a table in the back.

MARGE Ooch, there’s Krusty the Clown! Krusty, say something funny.

KRUSTY (TRAPPED) Uh... uh... there was this guy with a twelve inch... eh.. Congratulations Bob and Selma!

At the head table, Sideshow Bob grabs the microphone.

A smitten

SIDESHOW BOB Dear friends, forty one years ago, God took one hundred and sixty-eight pounds of clay and he made me a woman. (POINTS TO SELMA) And for this, I thank Him. Selma comes up to the microphone.

SELMA

I just wanted to say that as a wedding gift to my new husband, I’ve decided to give up smoking.
